setting.note: One night's haunting, walked across the City of London. Cornhill, the Royal Exchange and Bob Cratchit's Camden Town are named outright and still on the map; Scrooge's counting-house and Fezziwig's warehouse are unlocated interiors, pinned to the courts off Cornhill that tradition and Dickens's own clues point to. That split is exactly what the certainty labels are for.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Charles Dickens, first published 1843; the author died in 1870.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States and the United Kingdom, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1870).
rights.attribution: Charles Dickens, A Christmas Carol (1843). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The gold of the counting-house and of Scrooge's coins, the metal the whole fable is measured against. Clears WCAG AA on paper.
